pathways.expectationName: C2-Survey of Brit and/or Am. Lit (1860-present)
C2-Survey of Brit and/or Am. Lit (1860-present)

notes.content: The Comparative Literature major offers a flexible, multicultural, and interdisciplinary general education; this means students can take courses in numerous departments. The CL major is designed for undergraduates who wish to investigate both literature and language (from at least two different national traditions) within cultural, socioeconomic, historical, or political contexts. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end if spring before transfer: One course in English Composition (Expository Writing); One course in Critical Reading and Writing (Composition and Literature), Two survey courses in World or English literature (at least one must be world literature), and Two years of one Foreign Language. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https:// complit.ucla.edu and https:// admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
notes.contentHtml:
The Comparative Literature major offers a flexible, multicultural, and interdisciplinary general education; this means students can take courses in numerous departments. The CL major is designed for undergraduates who wish to investigate both literature and language (from at least two different national traditions) within cultural, socioeconomic, historical, or political contexts.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.


To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end if spring before transfer:

  • One course in English Composition (Expository Writing);
  • One course in Critical Reading and Writing (Composition and Literature),
  • Two survey courses in World or English literature (at least one must be world literature), and
  • Two years of one Foreign Language.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit https://complit.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.

notes.content: NOTE: Students must demonstrate a minimum of one year of proficiency in a foreign language, however completion of two years of a foreign language is strongly recommended as preparation for upper division coursework.
notes.contentHtml: NOTE: Students must demonstrate a minimum of one year of proficiency in a foreign language, however completion of two years of a foreign language is strongly recommended as preparation for upper division coursework.

notes.content: Completion of UCLA's English 10ABC series is not required for this major, however, these courses have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for the Comparative Literature major. All substitutions will be processed by the department after transfer. For additional information about this major visit https:// complit.ucla.edu .
notes.contentHtml: Completion of UCLA's English 10ABC series is not required for this major, however, these courses have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for the Comparative Literature major. All substitutions will be processed by the department after transfer. For additional information about this major visit https://complit.ucla.edu.

notes.content: The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 4 or higher on the AP English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for Writing I. The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep: A score of 5 or higher on the IB HL English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for Writing I.
notes.contentHtml:
The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 4 or higher on the AP English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for Writing I.


The IB Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the major prep:

  • A score of 5 or higher on the IB HL English Literature or Language exam will satisfy the requirement for Writing I.
